title: Suvarnadurg Fort
tagline: Suvarnadurg Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Suvarnadurg (translation: Golden Fort, also spelt Severndroog in English, a spelling sometimes also used for Savandurga) is a fort that is located on a small island in the Arabian
overview.description: Suvarnadurg (translation: Golden Fort, also spelt Severndroog in English, a spelling sometimes also used for Savandurga) is a fort that is located on a small island in the Arabian Sea, near Harnai in Konkan, in the Indian state of Maharashtra on the west coast of India between Mumbai and Goa. The literal meaning of Suvanadurga in the Marathi language is "Golden Fort" as it was considered as the pride or the "feather in the golden cap of Marathas". Built by Adilshah Navy for defence purposes, the fort also had a shipbuilding facility. The basic objective of establishing the fort was to counter

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Fattegad
topPlaces.description: Fattegad is a fort located 15 km from Dapoli, in the Ratnagiri district of Maharashtra. It is one of three forts built to guard the Suvarnadurg fort; the other two are Goa fort and Kanakdurg.

topPlaces.name: Goa Fort
topPlaces.description: Goa Fort is a fort located 15 km from Dapoli, in Ratnagiri district, of Maharashtra. This fort is one of the three forts built to guard the Suvarnadurg fort, the other two forts are Kanakdurg and Fattedurg.

topPlaces.name: Jog river
topPlaces.description: Jog River (Marathi: जोग नदी) is a river in Ratnagiri district of Maharashtra. The river has its origin in the Sahyadri ranges and flows westward near the town Dapoli and meets the Arabian Sea near Anjarle.

topPlaces.name: Anjarle
topPlaces.description: Anjarle is a village in Dapoli taluka, Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra India. It is a small port located near the mouth of Jog river, about 4 mi (6.4 km) south of Aade and 2 mi (3.2 km) north of Suvarnadurga.

topPlaces.name: Murud, Ratnagiri
topPlaces.description: Murud is a village in the Ratnagiri district of Maharashtra, India. It is 420.92 hectares in size.

topPlaces.name: Ade, Maharashtra
topPlaces.description: Ade is a small village in Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra state in Western India. The 2011 Census of India recorded 1,718 residents in the village.

topPlaces.name: Dalkhan, Maharashtra
topPlaces.description: Dalkhan is a small village in Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra state in Western India. The 2011 Census of India recorded a total of 123 residents in the village.

Murud is a village in the Ratnagiri district of Maharashtra, India. It is 420.92 hectares in size.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Suvarnadurg Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Suvarnadurg Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Suvarnadurg Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pune Airport (123 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (115 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Suvarnadurg Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Suvarnadurg Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Suvarnadurg Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Suvarnadurg Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Suvarnadurg Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Fattegad, Goa Fort, Jog river, Anjarle, Murud, Ratnagiri.
